Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:Qq]][[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Hanke (half houses)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.

Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:Qq]][[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Hanke (half houses)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.

Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|羽林家|" to "|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|"
revision.text:
[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:Qq]][[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}}

revision.comment: 63 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:名家 (公家) up to rev 96186194
revision.text:
'''名家'''（めいか）とは、[[鎌倉時代]]以降に成立した[[公家]]の[[家格]]のひとつで、[[羽林家]]と同列で、[[半家 (公家)|半家]]の上の序列に位置する。
== 概要 == [[大納言]]を[[極位極官|極官]]とするのは[[羽林家]]と同じだが、[[羽林家]]が[[近衛府|近衛中将]]などの武官職を経て[[大納言]]に進むのに対し、名家は[[侍従]]や[[弁官]]（[[蔵人]]・[[蔵人頭]]を兼任）などの文官職を経て[[中納言]]・[[大納言]]に進む。例外として[[日野家]]は[[左大臣]]に、[[烏丸家]]、[[万里小路家]]、[[清閑寺家]]は[[内大臣]]、[[柳原家]]は[[准大臣]]に進んだ者もある。また、[[日野流]]は[[侍従]]を初任とするが[[勧修寺流]]は[[衛門府|廷中佐（衛門権佐）]]を初任とする場合が多く、[[新家]]の場合には[[弁官]]のみで[[蔵人]]を兼ねず、途中で四府佐や八省輔を経た後に[[参議]]に任ぜられる家があるなど、家によって昇進に差がある場合がある。
[[幕末]]時で名家は30家弱を数えるが、その大部分は日野流[[藤原氏]]および[[勧修寺流]][[藤原氏]]および[[桓武平氏]]の諸家。中でも、[[日野家]]、[[広橋家]]、[[烏丸家]]、[[柳原家]]、[[竹屋家]]、[[裏松家]]、[[甘露寺家]]、[[葉室家]]、[[勧修寺家]]、[[万里小路家]]、[[清閑寺家]]、[[中御門家]]、[[坊城家]]は、'''十三名家'''と呼ばれる。
== 名家 == === 藤原北家日野流 === {{main|日野家}} [[藤原北家]]（[[藤原氏|藤原]][[朝臣]]）の[[日野家|日野流]]に12家： * 日野家：日野流[[嫡流]]。[[藤原真夏]]の後裔[[日野兼光]]の子[[日野資実|資実]]が祖。[[旧家]]。家業は[[儒道]]・[[歌道]]。 * [[広橋家]]：日野家支流。[[日野兼光]]の五男[[広橋頼資|頼資]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[文筆]]。 * [[柳原家]]：日野家支流。[[日野俊光]]の四男[[柳原資明|資明]]が祖。旧家。家業は文筆。 * [[烏丸家]]：日野家支流。[[日野資康|裏松資康]]の三男[[烏丸豊光|豊光]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[和歌]]。 * [[竹屋家]]：広橋[[庶家|庶流]]。[[広橋仲光]]の三男[[竹屋兼俊|兼俊]]が祖。旧家。 * [[日野西家]]：広橋庶流。[[広橋総光]]の三男[[日野西総盛|総盛]]が祖。[[新家]]。 * [[勘解由小路家 (日野流)|勘解由小路家]]：烏丸庶流。[[烏丸光広]]の二男[[勘解由小路資忠|資忠]]が祖。新家。家業は儒道。 * [[裏松家]]：烏丸庶流。[[烏丸光賢]]の二男[[裏松資清|資清]]が祖。新家。 * [[外山家]]：日野庶流。[[日野弘資]]の二男[[外山光顕|光顕]]が祖。新家。 * [[豊岡家]]：日野庶流。日野弘資の三男[[豊岡有尚|有尚]]が祖。新家。 * [[三室戸家]]：柳原庶流。[[柳原資行]]の子[[三室戸誠光|誠光]]が祖。新家。 * [[日野流北小路家|北小路家]]：三室戸庶流。[[三室戸誠光]]の子[[北小路徳光|徳光]]が祖。新家。
=== 藤原北家勧修寺流=== {{main|勧修寺流}} 藤原北家（藤原朝臣）の[[勧修寺家|勧修寺流]]に13家： * [[甘露寺家]]：勧修寺流[[嫡流]]。[[藤原高藤]]の曾孫[[藤原為輔|為輔]]が祖。旧家。家業は笛・儒道。 * [[葉室家]]：甘露寺支流。[[藤原為房]]の子[[葉室顕隆|顕隆]]が祖。旧家。 * [[勧修寺家]]：甘露寺支流。[[甘露寺資経]]の子[[観修寺経俊|経俊]]が祖。旧家。家業は儒道。 * [[万里小路家]]：甘露寺支流。甘露寺資経の子[[万里小路資通|資通]]が祖。旧家。 * [[清閑寺家]]：甘露寺支流。[[甘露寺経長]]の子[[清閑寺資房|資房]]が祖。旧家。 * [[中御門家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺経俊]]の子[[中御門経継|経継]]が祖。旧家。 * [[坊城家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺定資]]の子[[坊城俊実|俊実]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[紀伝道]]。俗に小川坊城と号す。 * [[芝山家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺光豊]]の子[[芝山宣豊|宣豊]]が祖。新家。 * [[池尻家]]：清閑寺庶流。[[清閑寺共房]]の子[[池尻共孝|共孝]]が祖。新家。 * [[梅小路家]]：清閑寺庶流。清閑寺共房の子[[梅小路定矩|定矩]]が祖。新家。 * [[岡崎家]]：中御門庶流。[[中御門尚良]]の子[[岡崎宣持|宣持]]が祖。新家。 * [[穂波家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺経広]]の子[[穂波経広|経広]]が祖。新家。元は海住山と号す。 * [[堤家]]：甘露寺庶流。[[甘露寺嗣長]]の子[[堤貞長|中川貞長]]が祖。その子[[堤輝長|輝長]]より堤と改める。新家。
=== 桓武平氏高棟王流 === {{main|平氏#高棟王流}} [[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]の[[平氏#桓武平氏|平朝臣高棟王流]]に3家： * [[平松家]]：[[西洞院家|西洞院]]庶流。[[西洞院時慶]]の子[[平松時庸|時庸]]が祖。新家。 * [[長谷家]]：西洞院家庶流。西洞院時慶の子[[長谷忠康|忠康]]が祖。新家。 * [[交野家]]：西洞院家庶流。西洞院時慶の子[[交野時貞|時貞]]が祖。新家。
== 関連項目 == * [[公卿]] * [[堂上家]]
{{堂上家一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めいけ}} [[Category:名家 (公家)|*]] [[Category:鎌倉時代]] [[Category:公家]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[名家 (公家)]] to [[Mei families]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''名家'''（めいか）とは、[[鎌倉時代]]以降に成立した[[公家]]の[[家格]]のひとつで、[[羽林家]]と同列で、[[半家 (公家)|半家]]の上の序列に位置する。
== 概要 == [[大納言]]を[[極位極官|極官]]とするのは[[羽林家]]と同じだが、[[羽林家]]が[[近衛府|近衛中将]]などの武官職を経て[[大納言]]に進むのに対し、名家は[[侍従]]や[[弁官]]（[[蔵人]]・[[蔵人頭]]を兼任）などの文官職を経て[[中納言]]・[[大納言]]に進む。例外として[[日野家]]は[[左大臣]]に、[[烏丸家]]、[[万里小路家]]、[[清閑寺家]]は[[内大臣]]、[[柳原家]]は[[准大臣]]に進んだ者もある。また、[[日野流]]は[[侍従]]を初任とするが[[勧修寺流]]は[[衛門府|廷中佐（衛門権佐）]]を初任とする場合が多く、[[新家]]の場合には[[弁官]]のみで[[蔵人]]を兼ねず、途中で四府佐や八省輔を経た後に[[参議]]に任ぜられる家があるなど、家によって昇進に差がある場合がある。
[[幕末]]時で名家は30家弱を数えるが、その大部分は日野流[[藤原氏]]および[[勧修寺流]][[藤原氏]]および[[桓武平氏]]の諸家。中でも、[[日野家]]、[[広橋家]]、[[烏丸家]]、[[柳原家]]、[[竹屋家]]、[[裏松家]]、[[甘露寺家]]、[[葉室家]]、[[勧修寺家]]、[[万里小路家]]、[[清閑寺家]]、[[中御門家]]、[[坊城家]]は、'''十三名家'''と呼ばれる。
== 名家 == === 藤原北家日野流 === {{main|日野家}} [[藤原北家]]（[[藤原氏|藤原]][[朝臣]]）の[[日野家|日野流]]に12家： * 日野家：日野流[[嫡流]]。[[藤原真夏]]の後裔[[日野兼光]]の子[[日野資実|資実]]が祖。[[旧家]]。家業は[[儒道]]・[[歌道]]。 * [[広橋家]]：日野家支流。[[日野兼光]]の五男[[広橋頼資|頼資]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[文筆]]。 * [[柳原家]]：日野家支流。[[日野俊光]]の四男[[柳原資明|資明]]が祖。旧家。家業は文筆。 * [[烏丸家]]：日野家支流。[[日野資康|裏松資康]]の三男[[烏丸豊光|豊光]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[和歌]]。 * [[竹屋家]]：広橋[[庶家|庶流]]。[[広橋仲光]]の三男[[竹屋兼俊|兼俊]]が祖。旧家。 * [[日野西家]]：広橋庶流。[[広橋総光]]の三男[[日野西総盛|総盛]]が祖。[[新家]]。 * [[勘解由小路家 (日野流)|勘解由小路家]]：烏丸庶流。[[烏丸光広]]の二男[[勘解由小路資忠|資忠]]が祖。新家。家業は儒道。 * [[裏松家]]：烏丸庶流。[[烏丸光賢]]の二男[[裏松資清|資清]]が祖。新家。 * [[外山家]]：日野庶流。[[日野弘資]]の二男[[外山光顕|光顕]]が祖。新家。 * [[豊岡家]]：日野庶流。日野弘資の三男[[豊岡有尚|有尚]]が祖。新家。 * [[三室戸家]]：柳原庶流。[[柳原資行]]の子[[三室戸誠光|誠光]]が祖。新家。 * [[日野流北小路家|北小路家]]：三室戸庶流。[[三室戸誠光]]の子[[北小路徳光|徳光]]が祖。新家。
=== 藤原北家勧修寺流=== {{main|勧修寺流}} 藤原北家（藤原朝臣）の[[勧修寺家|勧修寺流]]に13家： * [[甘露寺家]]：勧修寺流[[嫡流]]。[[藤原高藤]]の曾孫[[藤原為輔|為輔]]が祖。旧家。家業は笛・儒道。 * [[葉室家]]：甘露寺支流。[[藤原為房]]の子[[葉室顕隆|顕隆]]が祖。旧家。 * [[勧修寺家]]：甘露寺支流。[[甘露寺資経]]の子[[観修寺経俊|経俊]]が祖。旧家。家業は儒道。 * [[万里小路家]]：甘露寺支流。甘露寺資経の子[[万里小路資通|資通]]が祖。旧家。 * [[清閑寺家]]：甘露寺支流。[[甘露寺経長]]の子[[清閑寺資房|資房]]が祖。旧家。 * [[中御門家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺経俊]]の子[[中御門経継|経継]]が祖。旧家。 * [[坊城家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺定資]]の子[[坊城俊実|俊実]]が祖。旧家。家業は[[紀伝道]]。俗に小川坊城と号す。 * [[芝山家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺光豊]]の子[[芝山宣豊|宣豊]]が祖。新家。 * [[池尻家]]：清閑寺庶流。[[清閑寺共房]]の子[[池尻共孝|共孝]]が祖。新家。 * [[梅小路家]]：清閑寺庶流。清閑寺共房の子[[梅小路定矩|定矩]]が祖。新家。 * [[岡崎家]]：中御門庶流。[[中御門尚良]]の子[[岡崎宣持|宣持]]が祖。新家。 * [[穂波家]]：勧修寺庶流。[[観修寺経広]]の子[[穂波経広|経広]]が祖。新家。元は海住山と号す。 * [[堤家]]：甘露寺庶流。[[甘露寺嗣長]]の子[[堤貞長|中川貞長]]が祖。その子[[堤輝長|輝長]]より堤と改める。新家。
=== 桓武平氏高棟王流 === {{main|平氏#高棟王流}} [[平氏#桓武平氏|桓武平氏]]の[[平氏#桓武平氏|平朝臣高棟王流]]に3家： * [[平松家]]：[[西洞院家|西洞院]]庶流。[[西洞院時慶]]の子[[平松時庸|時庸]]が祖。新家。 * [[長谷家]]：西洞院家庶流。西洞院時慶の子[[長谷忠康|忠康]]が祖。新家。 * [[交野家]]：西洞院家庶流。西洞院時慶の子[[交野時貞|時貞]]が祖。新家。
== 関連項目 == * [[公卿]] * [[堂上家]]
{{堂上家一覧}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:めいけ}} [[Category:名家 (公家)|*]] [[Category:鎌倉時代]] [[Category:公家]]

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:Qq]][[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.

Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
