[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==

* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōjō Families list|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Mei families]][[Category:qq]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Mei families]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Mei families]][[Category:qq]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|court nobility (kuge)|ja|公家|1=Kuge|qq=|qq=}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|ja|中納言|qq=}} and {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the {{ill|Sadaijin (Minister of the Left)|ja|左大臣|1=Minister of the Left|qq=}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the {{ill|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|ja|内大臣|qq=}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo|ja|蔵人|qq=}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi|ja|参議|1=Sangi (Japan)|lt=Sangi|2=ja|3=参議|4=ru|5=Санги|6=fr|7=Sangi_(Japon)|8=ko|9=참의_(일본사)|10=es|11=Sangi|qq=}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|ja|幕末|qq=}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * {{ill|Yanagihara family|ja|柳原家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagihara Sukiyori|ja|柳原資明|qq=}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|waka poetry|ja|和歌|qq=}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原北家|qq=}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|ja|藤原高藤|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō (Japanese court nobles)|ja|公卿|1=Kugyō|qq=}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
[[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[ja:名家 (公家)]][[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Mei families]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Mei families]][[Category:qq]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the [[:en:Kuge|Kuge]] {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|qq=}} established from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]] onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|qq=}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]] differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|qq=}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|qq=}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} (often concurrently holding positions such as [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]] or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|qq=}}) before attaining the ranks of [[:en:Chūnagon|Chūnagon]] and [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]]. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}} who reached the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]], and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}} who advanced to the [[:en:Naidaijin|Udaijin (Minister of the Right)]], as well as the [[:en:Yanagiwara family|Yanagihara family]] whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|qq=}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|qq=}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|qq=}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|qq=}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=Benkan (administrator)|lt=Benkan|qq=}} without concurrently serving as [[:en:Kurōdo-dokoro|Kurōdo]], and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|qq=}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|qq=}} before being appointed [[:en:Sangi (Japan)|Sangi]], demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the [[:en:Bakumatsu|Bakumatsu]] period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}, [[:en:Yanagiwara family|Yanagihara family]], {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan]] in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|qq=}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|qq=}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|qq=}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|qq=}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|qq=}}. * [[:en:Yanagiwara family|Yanagihara family]]: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by [[:en:Yanagiwara Sukeakira|Yanagihara Sukiyori]], fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|qq=}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|qq=}}. An old family specializing in [[:en:Waka (poetry)|waka poetry]]. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|qq=}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|qq=}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|qq=}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|qq=}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the [[:en:Fujiwara Hok-ke|Northern House of the Fujiwara clan]] in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|qq=}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|qq=}}, great-grandson of [[:en:Fujiwara no Takafuji|Fujiwara no Takafuji]]. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|qq=}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|qq=}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|qq=}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|qq=}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|qq=}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|qq=}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|qq=}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|qq=}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|qq=}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|qq=}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|qq=}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|qq=}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|qq=}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * [[:en:Kugyō|Kugyō]] * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}}
