[[ja:名家 (公家)]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|WD=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|1=Kakaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11455533}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|1=highest official rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541435}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11638974}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2948088}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621873}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|WD=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} who reached the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353928}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561446}} who advanced to the {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|WD=Q1185338|lt=Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|1=Naidaijin}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|WD=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|1=jun-daijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395386}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2948088}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|1=shinke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11501762}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3270768}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|WD=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|WD=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu|1=Bakumatsu}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|WD=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127321}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22130269}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574311}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620727}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353928}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561446}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365015}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425648}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510579}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|1=Sukezane Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17225183}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|1=Fujiwara no Manatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623425}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485464}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11485470}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623078}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筆記|1=Writing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q37260}}. * {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|WD=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|en|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|WD=Q11534160|lt=Yanagihara Sukiyori|1=Yanagiwara Sukeakira}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|1=Hino Toshimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510566}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22126158}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|1=Q108783234|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108783234}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野資康|1=Sukeyasu Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q47470090}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|WD=Q737148|lt=waka poetry|1=Waka (poetry)}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22127321}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|1=Hirohashi Nakamitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20042593}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|1=Q11510632|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11510632}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|1=Kadenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400106}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|1=Q11400105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400105}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|1=Karasumaru Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11567199}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 儒教|1=Confucianism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9581}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22130269}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|1=Toyama family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430425}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|1=Toyama Mitsuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11430420}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|1=Toyooka family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22131470}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|1=Q11355137|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355137}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|1=Q22129005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22129005}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|1=Q111170803|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111170803}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|WD=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400605}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574311}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|1=Fujiwara no Tamesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623396}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|en|Fujiwara no Takafuji|WD=Q5507544|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|1=Fujiwara no Takafuji}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11620727}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原顕隆|1=Fujiwara no Akitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623648}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623393}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11400595}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353928}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|1=Sukemichi Madenokōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353935}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561446}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|1=Seikanji Sukefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15633041}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365015}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|1=Nakamikado Tsunetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11365022}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425648}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Q108855906|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q108855906}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604971}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|1=Q11614641|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11614641}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|1=Shibayama Nobutoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10565402}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|1=Q11551581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11551581}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|1=Umekōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538445}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|1=Q11472843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472843}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|1=Q11597057|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597057}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|1=Q11427982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11427982}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中川貞長}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|1=Tsutsumi Terunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110066555}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1079102}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|1=Hiramatsu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483074}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|1=Nishinotoin family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629027}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|1=Hiramatsu Tokitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483083}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|1=Tokiyoshi Nishinotōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11629028}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|1=Nagatani family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11653674}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|1=Q11374033|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374033}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.

[[ja:名家 (公家)]] [[ko:명가 (공가)]] '''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|1=Kakaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455533}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|1=highest official rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541435}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2948088}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|qid=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} who reached the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}} who advanced to the {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|1=Naidaijin}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|1=jun-daijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395386}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2948088}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|1=shinke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501762}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|qid=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|qid=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu|1=Bakumatsu}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485464}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22127321}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22130269}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574311}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620727}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400595}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365015}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425648}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623078}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|1=Sukezane Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17225183}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|1=Fujiwara no Manatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623425}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485464}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485470}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623078}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筆記|1=Writing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q37260}}. * {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|en|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|qid=Q11534160|lt=Yanagihara Sukiyori|1=Yanagiwara Sukeakira}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|1=Hino Toshimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510566}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|1=Q108783234|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108783234}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野資康|1=Sukeyasu Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q47470090}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|qid=Q737148|lt=waka poetry|1=Waka (poetry)}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22127321}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|1=Hirohashi Nakamitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20042593}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|1=Q11510632|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510632}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|1=Kadenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400106}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|1=Q11400105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400105}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|1=Karasumaru Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567199}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 儒教|1=Confucianism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9581}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22130269}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|1=Toyama family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430425}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|1=Toyama Mitsuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430420}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|1=Toyooka family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22131470}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|1=Q11355137|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355137}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|1=Q22129005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129005}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|1=Q111170803|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q111170803}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574311}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|1=Fujiwara no Tamesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623396}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|en|Fujiwara no Takafuji|qid=Q5507544|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|1=Fujiwara no Takafuji}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620727}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原顕隆|1=Fujiwara no Akitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623648}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623393}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400595}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|1=Sukemichi Madenokōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353935}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|1=Seikanji Sukefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15633041}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365015}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|1=Nakamikado Tsunetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365022}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425648}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Q108855906|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108855906}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604971}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|1=Q11614641|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614641}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|1=Shibayama Nobutoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10565402}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|1=Q11551581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551581}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|1=Umekōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11538445}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|1=Q11472843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11472843}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|1=Q11597057|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597057}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|1=Q11427982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427982}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中川貞長}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|1=Tsutsumi Terunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110066555}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|1=Hiramatsu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483074}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|1=Nishinotoin family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629027}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|1=Hiramatsu Tokitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483083}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|1=Tokiyoshi Nishinotōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629028}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|1=Nagatani family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653674}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|1=Q11374033|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374033}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
== Related topics == * {{ill|Kugyō|en|Kugyō|qid=Q3138036|lt=Kugyō|1=Kugyō}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke (upper noble houses)|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427847}} {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|名家 (公家)|version=96186194|comment=Imported full ja history}} <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11415356}} [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Kamakura period]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Mei families]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
'''名家''' (Meika) refers to one of the {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} {{ill|family ranks|ja|家格|1=Kakaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11455533}} established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} onwards, on the same level as the {{ill|Baronial Houses|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} and ranking above the {{ill|Half-house (Kuge)|ja|半家|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
While the {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} held the {{ill|gokui gokkan (highest rank and office)|ja|極位極官|1=highest official rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11541435}}, the same as the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}}, the path to becoming {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}} differed: members of the {{ill|Bairin-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|qq=}} typically advanced through military positions such as {{ill|Middle Captain of the Imperial Guards|ja|近衛中将|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} in the {{ill|Imperial Guard Division|ja|近衛府|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}}, whereas Meika members proceeded through civil posts like {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2948088}} or {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} (often concurrently holding positions such as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|qid=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}} or {{ill|Head of the Kurōdo|ja|蔵人頭|1=Kurōdonotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11621873}}) before attaining the ranks of {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chūnagon|1=Chūnagon}} and {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}. Exceptions include members of the {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} who reached the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}}, and individuals from the {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}, and {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}} who advanced to the {{ill|Naidaijin|en|Naidaijin|qid=Q1185338|lt=Udaijin (Minister of the Right)|1=Naidaijin}}, as well as the {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}} whose members became {{ill|Associate Minister|ja|准大臣|1=jun-daijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395386}}. Additionally, the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} typically began with the position of {{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍従|1=Chamberlain of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2948088}}, whereas the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}} often started at {{ill|Tenchūsa (Emon no Gensaku)|ja|廷中佐（衛門権佐）|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the case of newly established families {{ill|Shinke|ja|新家|1=shinke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501762}}, some only held civil posts like {{ill|Bengyō|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}} without concurrently serving as {{ill|Kurōdo-dokoro|en|Kurōdo-dokoro|qid=Q6447696|lt=Kurōdo|1=Kurōdo-dokoro}}, and certain families advanced through positions such as {{ill|Shifusa|ja|四府佐|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Hasshōho|ja|八省輔|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before being appointed {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|qid=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}, demonstrating variation in promotion paths.
By the end of the {{ill|Bakumatsu|en|Bakumatsu|qid=Q804558|lt=Bakumatsu|1=Bakumatsu}} period, there were just under thirty Meika families, most of which belonged to branches of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}—namely the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} and {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}}—as well as those of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}}. Among these, the following thirteen families are especially prominent and are collectively known as the '''Thirteen Meika''': {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}}, {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485464}}, {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}, {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}, {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22127321}}, {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22130269}}, {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574311}}, {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620727}}, {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400595}}, {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}, {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}}, {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365015}}, and {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425648}}.
== Meika ==
=== Fujiwara Hokke Hino-ryū === {{main|日野家|qq=}} Twelve families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Hino-ryū|ja|日野流|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}} branch:
* {{ill|Hino family|ja|日野家|1=Hino family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510579}}: the main line of Hino-ryū. Descended from {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623078}}, son {{ill|Hino Sukefusa|ja|日野資実|1=Sukezane Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17225183}}, descendant of {{ill|Fujiwara Manatsu|ja|藤原真夏|1=Fujiwara no Manatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623425}}. An old family focused on {{ill|Confucian studies and waka poetry|ja|儒道・歌道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hirohashi family|ja|広橋家|1=Q11485464|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485464}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Hirohashi Yorifusa|ja|広橋頼資|1=Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11485470}}, fifth son of {{ill|Hino Sukemitsu|ja|日野兼光|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原兼光|1=Q11623078|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623078}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|literary composition|ja|文筆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筆記|1=Writing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q37260}}. * {{ill|Yanagiwara family|en|Yanagiwara family|qid=Q11534139|lt=Yanagihara family|1=Yanagiwara family}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|en|Yanagiwara Sukeakira|qid=Q11534160|lt=Yanagihara Sukiyori|1=Yanagiwara Sukeakira}}, fourth son of {{ill|Hino Toshimitsu|ja|日野俊光|1=Hino Toshimitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510566}}. An old family specializing in literary composition. * {{ill|Karasuma family|ja|烏丸家|1=Karasumaru family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22126158}}: a cadet branch of the Hino family. Founded by {{ill|Karasuma Toyomitsu|ja|烏丸豊光|1=Q108783234|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108783234}}, third son of {{ill|Uramatsu Sukeyasu|ja|裏松資康|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日野資康|1=Sukeyasu Hino|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q47470090}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|qid=Q737148|lt=waka poetry|1=Waka (poetry)}}. * {{ill|Takeya family|ja|竹屋家|1=Q22127321|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22127321}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Takeya Kanetoshi|ja|竹屋兼俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Nakamitsu|ja|広橋仲光|1=Hirohashi Nakamitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20042593}}. An old family. * {{ill|Hino-nishi family|ja|日野西家|1=Q11510632|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510632}}: a cadet branch of Hirohashi. Founded by {{ill|Hino-nishi Sanemori|ja|日野西総盛|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hirohashi Tsunemitsu|ja|広橋総光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kagenyu Kōji family|ja|勘解由小路家|1=Kadenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400106}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Kagenu Suketada|ja|勘解由小路資忠|1=Q11400105|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400105}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsuhiro|ja|烏丸光広|1=Karasumaru Mitsuhiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11567199}}. A new family specializing in {{ill|Confucian studies|ja|儒道|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 儒教|1=Confucianism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9581}}. * {{ill|Uramatsu family|ja|裏松家|1=Q22130269|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22130269}}: a cadet branch of Karasuma. Founded by {{ill|Uramatsu Sukekiyo|ja|裏松資清|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, second son of {{ill|Karasuma Mitsukata|ja|烏丸光賢|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyama family|ja|外山家|1=Toyama family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430425}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyama Mitsuaki|ja|外山光顕|1=Toyama Mitsuaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430420}}, second son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Toyooka family|ja|豊岡家|1=Toyooka family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22131470}}: a cadet branch of Hino. Founded by {{ill|Toyooka Arihisa|ja|豊岡有尚|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, third son of {{ill|Hino Hiromoto|ja|日野弘資|qq=}}. A new family. * {{ill|Mimuroto family|ja|三室戸家|1=Q11355137|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11355137}}: a cadet branch of Yanagihara. Founded by {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Yanagihara Sukeyuki|ja|柳原資行|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Kōjunji family|ja|北小路家|1=Q22129005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22129005}}: a cadet branch of Mimuroto. Founded by {{ill|Kōjunji Tokumitsu|ja|北小路徳光|1=Q111170803|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q111170803}}, son of {{ill|Mimuroto Masamitsu|ja|三室戸誠光|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family.
=== Fujiwara Hokke Kanshūji-ryū === {{main|勧修寺流|qq=}} Thirteen families of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Northern House of the Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} in the {{ill|Kanshūji-ryū|ja|勧修寺流|1=Q11400605|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400605}} branch:
* {{ill|Kanroji family|ja|甘露寺家|1=Kanroji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574311}}: the main line of Kanshūji-ryū. Founded by {{ill|Fujiwara no Tameyosu|ja|藤原為輔|1=Fujiwara no Tamesuke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623396}}, great-grandson of {{ill|Fujiwara no Takafuji|en|Fujiwara no Takafuji|qid=Q5507544|lt=Fujiwara no Takafuji|1=Fujiwara no Takafuji}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|flute music and Confucian studies|ja|笛・儒道|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Hamuro family|ja|葉室家|1=Hamuro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620727}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Hamuro Akitaka|ja|葉室顕隆|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 藤原顕隆|1=Fujiwara no Akitaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623648}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamefusa|ja|藤原為房|1=Fujiwara no Tamefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623393}}. An old family. * {{ill|Kanshūji family|ja|勧修寺家|1=Kajūji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11400595}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunezane|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in Confucian studies. * {{ill|Marikōji family|ja|万里小路家|1=Madenokōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353928}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Marikōji Sukemichi|ja|万里小路資通|1=Sukemichi Madenokōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353935}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Suketsune|ja|甘露寺資経|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Seikanji family|ja|清閑寺家|1=Q11561446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561446}}: a branch of the Kanroji family. Founded by {{ill|Seikanji Sukefusa|ja|清閑寺資房|1=Seikanji Sukefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15633041}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsunaga|ja|甘露寺経長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Nakamikomado family|ja|中御門家|1=Nakanomikado family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365015}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Nakamikomado Tsunetsugu|ja|中御門経継|1=Nakamikado Tsunetsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11365022}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経俊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. * {{ill|Bōjō family|ja|坊城家|1=Bōjō family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425648}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Bōjō Toshizane|ja|坊城俊実|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|1=Q108855906|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q108855906}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Sadazane|ja|観修寺定資|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family specializing in {{ill|historiography|ja|紀伝道|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604971}}, commonly called {{ill|Ogawa Bōjō|ja|小川坊城|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Shibayama family|ja|芝山家|1=Q11614641|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614641}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Shibayama Nobutaka|ja|芝山宣豊|1=Shibayama Nobutoyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10565402}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Mitsutoyo|ja|観修寺光豊|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Ikejiri family|ja|池尻家|1=Q11551581|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11551581}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Ikejiri Tomotaka|ja|池尻共孝|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Umekoji family|ja|梅小路家|1=Umekōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11538445}}: a cadet branch of Seikanji. Founded by {{ill|Umekoji Sadamune|ja|梅小路定矩|qq=}}, son of {{ill|Seikanji Tomofusa|ja|清閑寺共房|1=Seikanji Tomofusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561444}}. A new family. * {{ill|Okazaki family|ja|岡崎家|1=Q11472843|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11472843}}: a cadet branch of Nakamikomado. Founded by {{ill|Okazaki Nobumochi|ja|岡崎宣持|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Nakamikomado Hisayoshi|ja|中御門尚良|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family. * {{ill|Honami family|ja|穂波家|1=Q11597057|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597057}}: a cadet branch of Kanshūji. Founded by {{ill|Honami Tsunehiro|ja|穂波経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of {{ill|Kanshūji Tsunehiro|ja|観修寺経広|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family originally called {{ill|Kaijūzan|ja|海住山|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * {{ill|Tsutsumi family|ja|堤家|1=Q11427982|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427982}}: a cadet branch of Kanroji. Founded by {{ill|Tsutsumi Sadanao|ja|堤貞長|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中川貞長}}, son of {{ill|Kanroji Tsuchinaga|ja|甘露寺嗣長|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. His son {{ill|Tsutsumi Terunaga|ja|堤輝長|1=Tsutsumi Terunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q110066555}} later adopted the Tsutsumi name. A new family.
=== Kammu Heishi clan Takamune-ō-ryū === {{main|平氏#高棟王流|qq=}} Three families of the {{ill|Kammu Heishi clan|ja|桓武平氏|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}} in the {{ill|Takamune-ō-ryū|ja|平朝臣高棟王流|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} branch:
* {{ill|Hiramatsu family|ja|平松家|1=Hiramatsu family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483074}}: a cadet branch of the {{ill|Nishinotoin family|ja|西洞院家|1=Nishinotoin family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629027}}. Founded by {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokinobu|ja|平松時庸|1=Hiramatsu Tokitsune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483083}}, son of {{ill|Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi|ja|西洞院時慶|1=Tokiyoshi Nishinotōin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629028}}. A new family. * {{ill|Hase family|ja|長谷家|1=Nagatani family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653674}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Hase Tadayasu|ja|長谷忠康|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family. * {{ill|Katano family|ja|交野家|1=Q11374033|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11374033}}: a cadet branch of the Nishinotoin family. Founded by {{ill|Katano Tokisada|ja|交野時貞|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, son of Nishinotoin Tokiyoshi. A new family.
